skill.name: libreoffice-writer
skill.description: Create, edit, format, export, and extract LibreOffice Writer (.odt) documents via UNO automation with session-based editing, structured text targets, tables, images, lists, patch workflows, and snapshots.
skill.summary: Automate LibreOffice Writer document operations through UNO integration for programmatic document creation, editing, and export.
skill.author: dfk1352

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 175 patterns but evaluation confirms these are false positives. The skill legitimately uses subprocess to launch LibreOffice with hardcoded arguments, tempfile for secure temp directories, and importlib for module detection. All external command usage has no user input injection vectors. Risk is low due to filesystem and external command dependencies requiring LibreOffice installation.

low_findings.title: External Process Execution
low_findings.description: subprocess.Popen launches LibreOffice soffice binary. All arguments are hardcoded strings with no user input injection. This is legitimate automation of installed software.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review confirms all subprocess arguments are hardcoded - soffice path is validated, all flags are static strings like --headless and --invisible.

low_findings.title: Temporary Directory Creation
low_findings.description: Uses tempfile.mkdtemp() to create isolated profile directories for LibreOffice instances. Directories are cleaned up in finally blocks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Python tempfile.mkdtemp() is the secure standard library approach for temp directories, and cleanup is guaranteed via try/finally.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access
low_findings.description: Reads LIBREOFFICE_PROGRAM_PATH environment variable to locate UNO module. Value is used only for path resolution, not executed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variable is used only for sys.path insertion to find UNO module - no code execution or command injection possible.

content.value_statement: Manually editing documents is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates LibreOffice Writer operations through UNO integration, enabling programmatic document creation, editing, formatting, and export.
actual_capabilities: Create new Writer documents programmatically at specified paths
Edit documents via session-based API with structured text targeting
Format text with bold, italic, underline, fonts, colors, and alignment
Insert, update, and delete tables with structured data
Insert, update, and delete images with size control
Create and modify ordered and unordered lists with nesting support
Apply patch workflows for atomic or best-effort batch operations
Export documents to PDF and DOCX formats
Capture page snapshots as PNG images for verification
limitations: Requires LibreOffice installation with Python UNO support on the system
All file paths must be absolute paths, not relative paths
Text targeting requires distinctive anchor text or explicit occurrence indices
Paragraph-level formatting applies to entire paragraphs containing matches
Cannot edit password-protected or corrupted documents

use_cases.title: Automated Report Generation
use_cases.description: Generate standardized reports by creating documents, inserting formatted text and tables, then exporting to PDF for distribution.
use_cases.target_user: Business analysts creating weekly performance reports

use_cases.title: Document Template Processing
use_cases.description: Load template documents, replace placeholder text with dynamic content, update tables with new data, and export customized versions.
use_cases.target_user: Legal teams generating contracts from templates

use_cases.title: Batch Document Operations
use_cases.description: Apply consistent formatting changes across multiple documents using patch workflows with atomic rollback on failures.
use_cases.target_user: Documentation managers updating style guides across repositories

prompt_templates.title: Create and Populate Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new LibreOffice Writer document at /path/to/report.odt. Insert a title "Monthly Report", add a section heading "Summary", then insert a paragraph describing this month's achievements. Export the final document to PDF.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Basic document creation and export workflow

prompt_templates.title: Edit with Structured Targeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Open /path/to/draft.odt and find the sentence "Quarterly revenue grew significantly" that appears after "Financial Overview" and before "Next Steps". Make it bold and centered. Then insert a bulleted list after "Action Items" with three tasks: Review budget, Schedule meeting, Send updates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Using WriterTarget with anchors and bounds

prompt_templates.title: Table Operations with Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Open the document at /path/to/data-report.odt. Insert a table with 4 rows and 3 columns named "Sales Data". Populate it with headers [Region, Q1, Q2] and data rows for North, South, East regions. Later update the Q2 values when new numbers arrive.

prompt_templates.title: Atomic Patch Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply this patch to /path/to/manuscript.odt in atomic mode: First, replace all instances of "Draft" with "Final". Second, format the chapter titles bold with 14pt font. Third, insert a table of contents. If any operation fails, rollback all changes and report the error.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Complex patch with atomic transaction semantics

output_examples.input: Create a new document with a title and export to PDF
Find and format text in existing document

best_practices: Always use absolute file paths when working with UNO-based Writer APIs
Anchor text targets with full sentences or paragraph-level phrases, not single words
Use after and before bounds to constrain searches when text may appear multiple times
Wrap session operations in try/finally or context managers to ensure proper cleanup
Test patch workflows in best_effort mode before switching to atomic for production
anti_patterns: Using relative paths - UNO requires absolute file paths for all document operations
Omitting occurrence parameter when the same text appears multiple times in a document
Calling session methods after session.close() has been invoked
Supplying malformed JSON in patch items or data fields
Expecting paragraph alignment to apply only to matched text instead of the full paragraph

faq.question: Why does the skill require LibreOffice to be installed?
faq.answer: This skill uses LibreOffice's UNO (Universal Network Objects) API to interact with Writer documents. UNO is LibreOffice's native automation interface, requiring a local LibreOffice installation with Python UNO bindings.

faq.question: What file formats can I export to?
faq.answer: The skill supports exporting to PDF and DOCX formats out of the box. Additional formats may be available depending on your LibreOffice installation and filter availability.

faq.question: How do I fix 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named uno'?
faq.answer: Add your system's LibreOffice Python path to PYTHONPATH. On Linux, this is typically /usr/lib/python3/dist-packages. Set PYTHONPATH to include both the skill scripts directory and the LibreOffice Python modules.

faq.question: What is the difference between atomic and best_effort patch modes?
faq.answer: Atomic mode stops on the first failure, resets the session, and persists nothing. Best_effort mode continues through all operations and records which succeeded, persisting successful changes even if some operations fail.

faq.question: Can I edit documents that are currently open in LibreOffice?
faq.answer: No. Documents should be closed before editing via UNO. Attempting to open a document already locked by another LibreOffice instance will fail or cause conflicts.

faq.question: How do I target specific text when the same phrase appears multiple times?
faq.answer: Use the occurrence field to specify which match to target (0-indexed), or add after and before bounds to narrow the search window to a specific section of the document.
